title: W3 Ignite Model Test - Set 14 (Raw Coding, DSA & Algorithms)

questions.text: What is the time complexity of building a Binary Max-Heap from an unsorted array of N elements using the optimal bottom-up approach?
questions.Explanation: While inserting elements one by one takes O(N log N), the `heapify` operation applied bottom-up takes O(N) time.

questions.text: Which bitwise operation can be used to quickly check if a positive integer 'x' is a power of 2?
questions.Explanation: Powers of 2 have only one set bit. Subtracting 1 flips all bits after that set bit (including itself). Bitwise ANDing them yields 0.

questions.text: In C++, what is the worst-case time complexity of searching for an element in `std::unordered_map`?
questions.Explanation: Average case is O(1), but in the worst case (many hash collisions), it degrades to O(N).

questions.text: What is the primary advantage of using an Object-Relational Mapper (ORM) in backend development?
options: It guarantees faster query execution than raw SQL
It maps database tables to classes, allowing developers to interact with the database using object-oriented paradigms
It automatically normalizes the database to 3NF
It replaces the need for a database engine

questions.text: Which sorting algorithm is NOT stable by default?
questions.Explanation: Quick Sort swaps non-adjacent elements, which can change the relative order of equal elements.

questions.text: In graph theory, which algorithm is best suited to find the shortest path from a single source to all other vertices in a graph with NO negative weight edges?
What does the C++ GCC compiler function `__builtin_popcount(x)` do?
options: Counts the number of leading zeros
Counts the number of trailing zeros
Counts the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation
Reverses the bits of the integer

questions.text: In Dynamic Programming, what are the two main properties a problem must have to be solvable using DP?
What is the output of `print(0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3)` in Python?
questions.Explanation: Due to floating-point arithmetic precision issues in IEEE 754, 0.1 + 0.2 evaluates to 0.30000000000000004.

questions.text: What is the space complexity of a recursive Depth-First Search (DFS) on a perfectly balanced binary tree with N nodes?
questions.Explanation: The space complexity is determined by the maximum depth of the call stack, which is O(log N) for a balanced tree.

questions.text: In JavaScript, what will `[1, 2, 10, 20].sort()` return?
questions.Explanation: By default, JS `sort()` converts elements to strings and compares their UTF-16 code unit values. '10' comes before '2'.

questions.text: When writing a test case generator for a graph problem, what is the maximum number of edges in a simple undirected graph with V vertices?
What is the primary cause of the 'N+1 query problem' when using an ORM?
options: The database crashing after N queries
Executing one query to fetch N records, and then N additional queries to fetch related data for each record
A syntax error in the SELECT statement

questions.text: Which data structure is typically used to implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache?
questions.Explanation: Hash Map provides O(1) access, and the Doubly Linked List provides O(1) removal and insertion to keep track of recency.

questions.text: What is the time complexity of querying the sum of a range [L, R] using a standard Segment Tree?
In Python, which of the following is the fastest way to check if an element exists in a large collection?
questions.Explanation: Sets use hash tables, making membership testing O(1) on average, compared to O(N) for lists and tuples.

questions.text: What is the output of `5 ^ 5` in C/C++, Java, or JS?
questions.Explanation: The caret (^) is the bitwise XOR operator. XORing a number with itself always results in 0.

questions.text: If a function calls itself as its very last action, what compiler optimization can prevent stack overflow?
Which algorithm is used to find the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a graph by sorting all edges and adding them one by one while avoiding cycles?
In PHP, what is the time complexity of `isset($array[$key])`?
questions.Explanation: PHP arrays are implemented as hash tables, so key lookups take O(1) time on average.

questions.text: Which of the following problems is classic for applying the 'Sliding Window' technique?
options: Finding the shortest path in a graph
Finding the maximum sum of a contiguous subarray of size K
Finding the prime factors of a number

questions.text: What is 'memoization' in the context of algorithms?
options: Caching the results of expensive function calls to avoid repeating the same calculations
Storing data in RAM instead of a hard drive
Converting a recursive function to an iterative one

questions.text: In C++, `std::vector::push_back` has an amortized time complexity of O(1). What happens internally when its capacity is reached?
options: It allocates a new memory block (usually double the size), copies old elements, and deletes the old block
It links a new block of memory via a pointer
It silently drops the new element

questions.text: What is the expected height of a randomly built Binary Search Tree with N distinct keys?
In JavaScript, what is the output of `console.log(Math.max())`?
questions.Explanation: With no arguments, Math.max() returns -Infinity so that any subsequent number compared to it will be larger.

questions.text: Which traversal of a Binary Search Tree (BST) visits the nodes in ascending sorted order?
What is the time complexity of the Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) algorithm for string matching? (N = length of text, M = length of pattern)
When solving the '0/1 Knapsack' problem using DP, what do the dimensions of the DP table typically represent?
options: Rows: Items considered, Columns: Current capacity
Rows: Current capacity, Columns: Total value
Rows: Total value, Columns: Item weights
Rows: Item weights, Columns: Item values

questions.text: In Python, how is memory managed for objects when they are no longer referenced?
options: Reference counting combined with a cycle-detecting garbage collector
Memory is never freed until the program exits

questions.text: Which operator is heavily used in a 'Fenwick Tree' (Binary Indexed Tree) to quickly find the parent/child index?
questions.Explanation: `i & -i` isolates the lowest set bit of `i`, which is crucial for traversing a Fenwick Tree.

questions.text: In SQL, what is the main risk of concatenating user input directly into a database query string?
What is the worst-case time complexity of Quick Sort?
questions.Explanation: If the pivot chosen is consistently the smallest or largest element (e.g., already sorted array with naive pivot selection), it degrades to O(N^2).

questions.text: Which data structure is naturally used to evaluate a postfix expression (Reverse Polish Notation)?
What does a 'Promise' represent in JavaScript?
options: A guarantee that an error will not occur
The eventual completion (or failure) of an asynchronous operation and its resulting value

questions.text: In C++, what does `std::unique` do when applied to a vector?
options: Removes all duplicate elements from the entire vector
Removes consecutive duplicate elements, returning an iterator to the new end
Throws an error if there are duplicates
Sorts the vector and removes duplicates
questions.Explanation: It only removes consecutive duplicates. You must sort the vector first if you want to remove all duplicates.

questions.text: If a hash table uses chaining to resolve collisions, what is the data structure at each bucket?
What is the base case of the classic recursive Fibonacci function `fib(n)`?
Which graph algorithm relies heavily on a Disjoint Set Union (DSU) / Union-Find data structure?
In PHP, what is the difference between `==` and `===`?
options: `==` checks value equality after type juggling, `===` checks both value and type strict equality

questions.text: What does 'Eager Loading' attempt to solve in the context of an ORM?
questions.Explanation: Eager loading uses JOINs or subsequent IN queries to fetch all related records upfront, avoiding N+1 separate queries.

questions.text: In Python, what is a decorator?
options: A function that takes another function and extends its behavior without explicitly modifying it
A class that inherits from multiple parents
A specific type of list comprehension

questions.text: What is the time complexity of finding the diameter of a tree using the 'Two BFS/DFS' method?
questions.Explanation: Running two standard traversals takes linear time relative to the number of vertices and edges.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of an NP-Complete problem?
What is the purpose of the `volatile` keyword in C/C++?
options: To make a variable accessible across threads
To prevent the compiler from optimizing code related to that variable because its value may change outside the program's control

questions.text: In JS, how does `setTimeout(fn, 0)` affect the execution of `fn`?
options: Executes immediately, blocking the main thread
Puts it in the callback queue to be executed after the current execution stack clears
Runs it on a separate background thread

questions.text: What is a 'Trie' data structure primarily used for?
options: Efficient string matching and prefix searches (like autocomplete)

questions.text: Which paradigm does Dart heavily utilize for its UI layout rendering in Flutter?
What is 'Polymorphism' in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: Creating new classes from existing ones
The ability of different objects to respond to the same method call in their own specific way

questions.text: In SQL, what is the result of joining a table with itself?
If you need to update elements in a range `[L, R]` and query point values efficiently, which array-based structure is ideal?
questions.Explanation: A Difference Array allows O(1) range updates. You can reconstruct the point values by calculating the prefix sum of the difference array.
